groups.question_title: Which TWO points do the students most frequently make about their music background before entering music schools?

list.title: C
list.content: they played more than one instrument

groups.question_title: Which TWO points do the students most frequently make about their music practice?

list.title: D
list.content: they did not have enough practice

list.title: E
list.content: they enjoyed the challenge of playing complex pieces

groups.question_title: Which TWO points do the students most frequently make about playing pieces that would be assessed?

list.title: A
list.content: they liked to hear others playing the same piece

list.title: B
list.content: they wanted to play unknown pieces

list.title: C
list.content: they played a piece by memory

list.title: D
list.content: they played a piece selected by the tutor

groups.question_title: What comment do the students make about the following practice?
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What comment do the students make about the following practice?
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 27-30.

list.title: B
list.content: it is challenging but also enjoyable

list.title: F
list.content: need to work effectively with others

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Hey Gabriella, how is it going?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: I've been pretty busy lately.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Oh, what are you up to?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: I've been working on a dissertation on music education specializing in music performance.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Sounds intriguing.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: What have you found?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Well, I interviewed 18 freshmen majoring in music performance as my sample population.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: I first asked about their music background before being accepted to music schools.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Let me guess, have they all got relevant qualifications in music?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Well, even though there is a wealth of music qualifications available, most of the students didn't receive any until they entered music schools.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Some capabilities like music production or music technology are pretty tough going.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Now that you've mentioned it, I recall that I got mine in sound engineering in my sophomore year.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: But the majority of these students admitted that they had performed in front of a large crowd.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Around 80% of them had performed on stage as band members in secondary schools.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: A few of them played piano or guitar at the time.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: And it also surprised me that they were generally able to play multiple instruments with proficiency.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Have they tried to compose a piece of music before?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Only in rare cases did they have prior experience in composition.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: They would have to do it sooner or later.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: There is a whole module for it.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: What did they say about practising?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: All subjects mentioned that they feared being marked too low and tried their best.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: So they were like practising every single day?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Not for most people.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Some students worked with others in a band, so they had to arrange the time for rehearsals.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: But all 18 students had sufficient amount of practice.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: And they frequently mentioned that playing technical pieces was exhilarating.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: I feel that way, too.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: And then I asked questions about how they chose the pieces that were assessed and their attempts to improve their performance in recital classes.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: What did they say?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Two students mentioned that they always asked their tutors' advice on the pieces they were going to perform because they felt that would give them a competitive edge over those who had no clue of tutors' preference in music.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Well, that's a bit sneaky.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: But the most common remark was that they had a minimal interest in pieces that the public barely knew.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Instead, most prefer popular pieces that other students would also choose.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: How come?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: They said that this could mirror their weaknesses and thus help them to perfect the techniques for handling the most difficult bits.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: All but one of the 18 students admitted that to perform well, they had to learn the music by heart, or otherwise, they would have to divert their attention and thus make some errors.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: I agree.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Then I asked them about their thoughts on practising in different aspects.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: And some of their answers were entirely unexpected.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Oh, what aspects did you look into?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: I first asked how they feel about playing solo.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: What did they say?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: They said being a solo performer takes desire, focus and hard work.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: And the performance would not pull off if one took the stage without confidence.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: But they all took great pleasure in it.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Yeah, that's quite different from playing in an orchestra, where musicians have to work with other members to produce a harmonious sound that is pleasing to the ear.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Right.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: And I also asked whether the choice of the piece to play would affect their performance.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Well, that's a big yes for me.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: When I play a song I don't like, I won't devote myself to it.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: That's widely accepted.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: The audience can tell when performers are being authentic and inspired by the music they are performing.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Many would spend hours or even days choosing the right piece.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: You have to put a lot of thought into it before it's too late.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: So what kind of musical genre do they prefer?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: The answers vary vastly.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: But one thing in common is that they rarely switch from one style to another.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Of course.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: It is tough to make such a transition.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: Most instrumentalists initially start with some classical based training.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: But they don't know how to take their already existing knowledge and apply it to a new style of music.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: What about their feedback on playing duets?

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Yes, that's the last question on my list.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: I asked their take on playing a duet with someone with the same musical instrument, like a violin duet.

transcript.speaker: Gabriella
transcript.en: Although it is pretty demanding to do so, they all admitted that it is helpful in that it renews their motivation while enhancing their technical skills.

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: So, have you started writing the 1st draft of your dissertation?

transcript.speaker: Troy
transcript.en: I can help if...

21.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 music background, music schools；提示词为 most frequently, before entering music schools。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后男生提出“他们是否都有相关的资格证书”，为 B 选项的同义替换，女生表示没有，并说“他们直到上了音乐学院才获得”，可判断 B 选项不符合题干要求，可以排除。女生接着提到“大多数学生都承认他们曾公开表演过”，为 D 选项“当众演奏”的同义替换，可判断 D 选项为正确答案。题干用词虽然是“最频繁地”，但由于文本背景是对 18 名学生的调研，因此大多数人的回答就是最频繁的回答，注意结合语境来理解相应的替换。女生虽提及 piano 一词，但说的并不是 E 选项的“上过钢琴课”，注意不要盯着选项找字面信息听。女生之后又补充“他们大体上能够熟练地演奏多种乐器”，为 C 选项的同义替换，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。注意，女生说的 generally 是 most frequently 的同义替换。最后，男生用提问的方式提及 A 选项，女生对此表示否定，可排除 A 选项。

22.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 music background, music schools；提示词为 most frequently, before entering music schools。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后男生提出“他们是否都有相关的资格证书”，为 B 选项的同义替换，女生表示没有，并说“他们直到上了音乐学院才获得”，可判断 B 选项不符合题干要求，可以排除。女生接着提到“大多数学生都承认他们曾公开表演过”，为 D 选项“当众演奏”的同义替换，可判断 D 选项为正确答案。题干用词虽然是“最频繁地”，但由于文本背景是对 18 名学生的调研，因此大多数人的回答就是最频繁的回答，注意结合语境来理解相应的替换。女生虽提及 piano 一词，但说的并不是 E 选项的“上过钢琴课”，注意不要盯着选项找字面信息听。女生之后又补充“他们大体上能够熟练地演奏多种乐器”，为 C 选项的同义替换，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。注意，女生说的 generally 是 most frequently 的同义替换。最后，男生用提问的方式提及 A 选项，女生对此表示否定，可排除 A 选项。

23.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 music practice，条件为 most frequently。
题目解析：定位词在原文被替换为 practising，比较容易辨认。随后听到 C 选项被替换为“他们害怕评分过低，都尽了自己最大的努力”，符合题干要求，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。接着男生又问到 B 选项“他们是否每天练习”，女生对此表示否定，可排除 B 选项。女生之后又补充“所有学生都练习得很充分”，与 D 选项的表达相反，可排除 D 选项。最后，女生提及“他们经常提到演奏技巧型曲目很令人激动”，为 E 选项的同义替换，且符合题干要求，可判断 E 选项为正确答案。

24.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 music practice，条件为 most frequently。
题目解析：定位词在原文被替换为 practising，比较容易辨认。随后听到 C 选项被替换为“他们害怕评分过低，都尽了自己最大的努力”，符合题干要求，可判断 C 选项为正确答案。接着男生又问到 B 选项“他们是否每天练习”，女生对此表示否定，可排除 B 选项。女生之后又补充“所有学生都练习得很充分”，与 D 选项的表达相反，可排除 D 选项。最后，女生提及“他们经常提到演奏技巧型曲目很令人激动”，为 E 选项的同义替换，且符合题干要求，可判断 E 选项为正确答案。

25.analysis:
听前预测：条件为 most frequently, playing pieces that would be assessed。
题目解析：先听到女生提到“两名学生让导师给出选曲建议”，对应 D 选项，但不符合题干要求的 most frequently，可排除 D 选项。随后听到 B 选项被替换为“鲜为人知的曲目”，但搭配的信息为“兴趣不大”，两者态度相反，可排除 B 选项。继而听到“他们大多数人都偏爱其他人也会选的流行曲目”，为 A 选项的同义替换，且符合题干要求，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。最后，女生提及“17 名学生都承认要记住曲子才能表演好”，为 C 选项的同义替换，且符合题干要求，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

26.analysis:
听前预测：条件为 most frequently, playing pieces that would be assessed。
题目解析：先听到女生提到“两名学生让导师给出选曲建议”，对应 D 选项，但不符合题干要求的 most frequently，可排除 D 选项。随后听到 B 选项被替换为“鲜为人知的曲目”，但搭配的信息为“兴趣不大”，两者态度相反，可排除 B 选项。继而听到“他们大多数人都偏爱其他人也会选的流行曲目”，为 A 选项的同义替换，且符合题干要求，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。最后，女生提及“17 名学生都承认要记住曲子才能表演好”，为 C 选项的同义替换，且符合题干要求，因此 C 选项为正确答案。

27.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 play solo。
题目解析：通过定位词的拼写预判其大致发音，便于定位。定位词原词重现，随后听到女生提到“成为一名独奏表演者需要主动、专注和努力……他们都乐在其中”，为 B 选项的同义替换，可判断 B 选项为正确答案。注意，女生虽提及 confidence 一词，但并不是 C 选项的“（独奏能够）帮助增强信心”，而是“如果没有信心就登台，演出也不会成功”，可判断 C 选项偷换概念，可以排除。

28.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 choose a piece to play。
题目解析：上一题结束后，男生提及 D 选项，但说的是在管弦乐队中演奏的情况，与本题无关，可排除 D 选项。本题定位词以 the choice of the piece to play 的形式出现，随后听到女生提及“他们会花费数小时乃至数天”，为 E 选项的同义替换，可判断 E 选项为正确答案。

29.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 musical genres。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后女生提及“他们鲜有从一种风格转变为另一种”，为 G 选项的同义替换，可判断 G 选项为正确答案。

30.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 play duet。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，随后女生提及“虽然要求很高”，对应 B 选项的 challenging，继续往下听，听到“他们承认对于提高积极性和演奏技巧帮助很大”，为 A 选项的同义替换，可判断 A 选项为正确答案。之后原文并未提及 B 选项 enjoyable 这一信息，可排除 B 选项。
